description_long: This object consists of three stylized flowers, each with a distinct color and petal arrangement, mounted on slender green stems. The leftmost flower is a red, tulip-like bloom with tightly clustered, pointed petals forming a cup shape. The central flower is pink with five broad, rounded petals radiating outward in a flat, open whorl. The rightmost flower is yellow with six to seven rounded petals arranged in a radial pattern, forming a daisy-like appearance. All three flowers have simple, straight stems and lack visible leaves. The overall design is minimalistic and geometric, with smooth surfaces and clean edges. These flowers would be suitable for decorative use in urban parks, sidewalks, or public gardens.
description: A group of three stylized flowers—red, pink, and yellow—each with distinct petal shapes and green stems, arranged in a simple, geometric style.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three flowers side by side: red tulip-like on the left, pink whorled in the center, and yellow daisy-like on the right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the height difference and the layered arrangement of the flowers, with the red flower at the back and the yellow flower in front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the flowers, with the yellow flower now on the left, pink in the center, and red on the right.
description_view_3: Oblique view emphasizing the overlapping petals and the spatial relationship between the three flowers, with the red flower in the foreground.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The red flower has petals arranged in a tight, overlapping whorl forming a cup shape; the pink flower displays a radial arrangement with five broad petals spaced evenly; the yellow flower has a radial, daisy-like arrangement with slightly overlapping petals.
